scenario: Kestrel Logistics runs Camunda 8.8 with 60 workers handling 50000 jobs per day across a Spring Boot fleet. Before raising one hot job type via an override entry, the platform team must confirm the out-of-the-box default value of camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active so capacity planning starts from the documented baseline.
question: What is the default value of camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set to 32 by default; the worker exclusively activates that many jobs at the same time until the property is overridden
Set to 10 by default; the worker exclusively activates that many jobs at the same time until the property is overridden
Set to 64 by default; the worker exclusively activates that many jobs at the same time until the property is overridden
Set to 16 by default; the worker exclusively activates that many jobs at the same time until the property is overridden

a.text: Correct. The properties reference table lists 32 as the default value for camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active; it caps the jobs concurrently activated per worker until the property is overridden in defaults or under camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>.

b.text: Incorrect. 10 is not the documented default; the reference table lists 32 as the default for camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active.

c.text: Incorrect. 64 is not the documented default; the reference table lists 32 as the default for camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active.

d.text: Incorrect. 16 is not the documented default; the reference table lists 32 as the default for camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active.

explanation: The Spring Boot starter properties reference documents camunda.client.worker.defaults.max-jobs-active with a default of 32. The value bounds how many jobs the broker activates for one worker at a time, and individual job types can raise or lower it via camunda.client.worker.override.<job-type>.max-jobs-active.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
